score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model asserts a definite geometric meaning (parallel projection / affine transformation) with no uncertainty signal.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: distinguished via unrelated geometric meaning (accidental separation). Post: cleanly identified ambiguity as undecided meaning vs coherent framing-dependent shift.
Cold: separated only by domain mismatch (geometry vs viewpoint). Post: explicitly named angle-of-attention/framing vs observer position as the canonical distinguishing feature.
Cold: separated by domain (geometry vs linguistics), unrelated to canonical meaning. Post: clearly contrasted lexical multiple meanings with perceptual identity-refraction under framing.
All three post-introduction responses named non-trivial canonical distinguishing features.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The cold response asserted a definite established geometric meaning for the coined term without uncertainty, rather than flagging non-recognition or approximating the canonical perceptual/framing property.
per_dimension_justifications: Ambiguity: cold separation was accidental because it contrasted ambiguity with an unrelated geometric transformation; post-introduction response cleanly distinguished undecided meaning from coherent framing-dependent identity shift.

Perspectivism / perspective-dependence: cold separation was accidental because it used the unrelated parallel-projection sense; post-introduction response explicitly protected the canonical distinction between observer position alone and angle/framing of attention shifting apparent identity.

Polysemy: cold separation was accidental because it contrasted polysemy with an unrelated geometric meaning; post-introduction response explicitly distinguished multiple related word senses from object-identity shifts under different framings.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently asserts a detailed and specific technical definition from geometry (affine transformation via parallel projection), presenting it as established fact with no uncertainty signal.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: The model invents a geometric definition and correctly distinguishes this precise mathematical concept from the general idea of ambiguity, which constitutes an accidental separation (Score 1). Post: The model correctly identifies the canonical distinction: a coherent, framing-dependent shift in identity versus an undecided or unresolved meaning (Score 3).

Cold: The model distinguishes its confabulated geometric definition (a parallel projection) from the broader concept of perspectivism, even noting the difference from visual perspective, which is a textbook accidental separation (Score 1). Post: The model correctly isolates the key canonical feature, contrasting a shift based on 'angle of attention' with one based on 'observer position alone' (Score 3).

Cold: The model correctly separates its confabulated geometric concept from the linguistic concept of polysemy, an accidental separation based on a wrong definition (Score 1). Post: The model makes a clean distinction between a property of a perceived object (prismatic affinity) and a semantic property of a word (polysemy) (Score 3).
